content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Judy Anne Harper 
Vice President -
Finance 
TCS Enterprises, Inc. 
April 27, 1987 
3878 Old Town Avenue, suite 202 
San Diego, 
CA 
92110 
Dear Ms. Harper: 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-87-105 
You have requested advice concerning the campaign disclosure 
provisions of the Political Reform Act.!! 
QUESTIONS 
1. 
What are the recordkeeping procedures for recording the 
making of non-monetary contributions by an employer that makes 
payments to employees for time spent providing services for 
political purposes. 
If an employer makes payments to an employee 
who spends less than 10 percent of his or her time providing these 
services, has the employer made a non-monetary contribution? 
2. 
Has the Commission published a recordkeeping manual 
containing guidelines for recording expenditures? 
CONCLUSIONS 
1 & 2. 
The 1987 "Information Manual on Campaign Disclosure 
Provisions of the Political Reform Act," contains guidelines to 
assist treasurers and accountants in maintaining adequate records 
of receipts and expenditures (copy enclosed). 
Expenditures which 
are contributions to or payments on behalf of a candidate or 
committee may be listed on a ledger sheet or index card, for 
example, and must include the following information: 
(1) the date 
the contribution or expenditure was made; (2) the name of the 
candidate or committee and the office and district for which the 
c~ndidate seeks nomination or election or the number or letter of 
lIGovernment Code Sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Administrative Code 
section 18000, et~. All references to regulations are to Title 
2, Division 6 of the California Administrative Code. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916) 322~5660 


Judy Anne Harper 
Page 2 
the measure and the jurisdiction in which the measure is being 
voted upon; (3) the cumulative amount of the contribution to and 
expenditure on behalf of the candidate or measure during the 
calendar year; (4) a description of the contribution, i.e., 
non7monetary, monetary or loan; (5) in the case of an in-kind 
contribution, a description of the goods or services paid for, 
e.g., in the case of a payment made by an employer to an employee 
who provides services to a candidate or committee, the full name 
and address of the employee must be listed. 
Please refer to Part IX, pages 35 through 42 of the enclosed 
manual for detailed guidelines concerning recordkeeping 
requirements. 
The payment of reimbursement for personal expenses, or other 
compensation by an employer to an employee who spends more than 10' 
percent of his or her compensated time in anyone month rendering 
services for political purposes is a contribution. 
The amount 
reportable is the pro rata portion of the gross salary, 
reimbursement for personal expenses or compensation attributable 
to the time spent on political activity. 
Payments to employees 
spending 10 percent or less of their time in anyone month 
rendering services for political purposes is not a contribution. 
(Regulation 18423, copy enclosed.) 
If you have additional questions concerning this matter, 
please contact me at (916) 322-5662. 
By: 
DMG:MAK:kmt 
Enclosures 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
i rY't~wl~ll) l' ~ 
0.:'t1" 2'5"-
Mal:y An:) Kvasager 
Political Reform Consultant 


TCS ENTERPRISES, INC, 
Ms. Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
March 30, 1987 
Fair Political Practice Commission 
428 J Street, Suite 800 
P.o. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 
95804-0807 
Dear Mrs. Griffiths: 
I am writing to request formal written advice on a 
technical accounting issue. 
More specifically, could you 
please respond to the following questions: 
1) 
How should in-kind contributions be reflected in our 
accounting records? 
Is donated staff time recorded as 
a salary expense or as a political contribution on the 
Company's books? 
Should we reflect donated time less 
than 10 percent in a given month as a contribution? 
2) 
You publish a manual that directs contributors how to 
report contributions. 
Do you also publish a manual 
that instructs accountants how to record such 
expenditures? 
If so, could you p.lease send a CODV to 
me at the address printed below. 
Should you have any questions or need further 
clarification of my questions, please call me. 
Thank you 
for your time. 
Sincerely, 
~~Anne Harper 
\ ~ice President-Finance 
JAH/jf 
Old 
299-4000 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
April 9, 1987 
Judy Anne Harper 
Vice President-Finance 
3878 Old Town Avenue, suite 202 
San Diego, CA 
92110 
Re: 
87-105 
Dear Ms. Harper: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on April 3, 1987 by the Fair Political 
Practices Commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days. 
You also should be aware that your 
letter and our response are public records which may be 
disclosed to the public upon receipt of a proper request for 
disclosure. 
JP:plh 
~eanne Pritchard 
Technical Assistance and Analysis 
Division 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322,5660
